description: Claim-level typed correspondence layered on the existing Principia Atlas. Lean is the validity boundary; this registry records correspondence status without collapsing literary expression into theorem text.

status_vocabulary.interpretive: philosophical or synthetic reading grounded in but not identical to formal results
status_vocabulary.poetic: intentionally non-propositional material outside theorem certification

entries.claim: A normalized pure-state vector constructs a Hermitian rank-one density and lowers through a fixed observer response into the existing resolution certificate.
entries.atlas_note: The construction is forward. The observer certificate is not strengthened with an upstream invariant.

entries.claim: Global-phase-related vectors have the same pure-state density and the same fixed-kernel observer certificate; distinct normalized vectors collide under lowering.
entries.atlas_note: This is the proved non-injectivity of observer lowering.

entries.claim: An arbitrary complete-frame readout system reconstructs a Hermitian readout certificate.
entries.atlas_note: The implication is formally false, not awaiting proof.

entries.claim: An arbitrary normalized observer-resolution certificate forces Hermiticity or reconstructs the full upstream state.
entries.atlas_note: The failure is information loss across the observer boundary, not a missing certificate field.

entries.claim: A resolution certificate whose retained matrix is explicitly Hermitian constructs the existing Hermitian readout certificate.
premises: The retained reduced-state matrix is Hermitian.
entries.atlas_note: A completed local conditional construction; it is not full quantum Gleason.

entries.claim: Complex operator expectations scale by conjugate(a)*a and are invariant under certified global phase; real a^2 scaling is not the complex law.
entries.atlas_note: The exact kernel supports the phase-faithful correction without certifying the PS axiom as derived.

entries.claim: The appendix gives a human mathematical Gleason derivation under PS-C1--PS-C6; the Lean companion supplies bounded finite constructions and boundaries but does not certify this full theorem.
entries.atlas_note: The distinction preserves the book proof and prevents projection of the local conditional Hermitian construction as full quantum Gleason.

entries.claim: Zero-defect convergence identifies the Hilbert cross-section; Born identification additionally consumes a separate uniqueness certificate.
premises: Defect continuity and convergence to zero.
entries.atlas_note: Hilbert collapse alone does not select a probability functional.

entries.claim: Reading non-contextuality as the fixed-point condition of reflective collapse is an interpretive synthesis of the conditional theorem.
entries.atlas_note: The prose reach is preserved but not presented as a further kernel identity.

entries.claim: Born as the p=2 slice of a continuous observer geometry is the book's synthetic reading; deterministic-looking limits describe resolved appearance and do not invert the observer map.
entries.atlas_note: The scholium remains literary and synthetic while the reconstruction boundary is visible.

entries.claim: Operatio is intentional operator-poetry and a semantic carrier, not a proposition awaiting theorem certification.
entries.atlas_note: Formal silence does not reject or flatten the operator-poetic register.
